paper_title: Ring Attention with Blockwise Transformers for Near-Infinite Context

method_section.problem: Quadratic complexity with sequence length makes scaling Transformers to longer input sequences challenging
existing_solutions: Computing softmax matrix without materializing full matrix
Blockwise computation of self-attention and feedforward without approximations
method_section.key_challenge: Storing output of each layer due to self-attention's n to n interactions

proposed_solution.name: Ring Attention
advantages: Allows training up to device count times longer sequences than baselines
Enables training sequences that exceed millions in length
Doesn't add overheads to communication and computation

results_section.key_observation: Self-attention and feedforward network computations can be performed in a blockwise fashion, allowing distribution of sequence dimensions across multiple devices

method_details.outer_loop: Distributed among hosts, with each device managing its respective input block
method_details.inner_loop: Each device computes blockwise attention and feedforward operations specific to its designated input block
method_details.communication: Each device sends key-value blocks to the next device and receives from the previous one
method_details.efficiency: Overlapping block computations and transfers results in no added overhead

title: Ring Attention with Blockwise Transformers for Near-Infinite Context
